ui_lora_candidates.name: Human Interface 2.0

ui_lora_candidates.name: Game UI Essentials 2.0

prompts.scenario_ui:
Using the reference crop, recreate ONLY this asset as a clean 2D mobile game sprite.

Asset: Crown High Score Icon
Category: ui
Description: A small, golden crown icon with three points and a rounded base.
Gameplay role: Visual indicator for the high score section.

=== STYLE LOCK (must match exactly across every asset in this game) ===
Overall: Casual 2D vector-style puzzle game with a 'sweet' candy-like aesthetic, featuring soft beveled blocks and a warm chocolate-toned background.
Rendering technique: 2D vector-cartoon with clean gradients and soft inner glows.
Line work: Thin, subtle dark-brown outlines on blocks to provide depth; UI text uses thick brown drop shadows.
Palette: Warm and saturated, dominated by dusty pink, cream, chocolate brown, and golden yellow.
Shading: Soft cel-shading with subtle top-down gradients on blocks to create a tactile, 3D-beveled appearance.
Scale / proportions: Toy-like and tactile; blocks feel like physical plastic or candy tiles.
DO NOT use any of these styles: pixel art, photorealistic, 3D rendered, hand-drawn raster, gritty, neon, minimalist flat.
Match this style regardless of what the asset depicts — do not default to genre tropes (e.g. ninjas as pixel art, robots as voxel, fantasy as painterly) unless the lock above says so.

Preserve the source asset's silhouette, color family, proportions, camera angle, and readable details.
Improve video compression artifacts and make the asset crisp and production-quality.
Center the asset with comfortable transparent padding.

Remove everything that is not the asset: no background, no wall, no character, no hand cursor, no UI, no smoke/trail/impact particles unless explicitly part of the target.
Use a transparent background PNG when supported. If transparency is not preserved, use a plain simple background that can be removed cleanly.
Do not add new objects or change the gameplay read.

notes: Prefer HTML/CSS for simple counters/labels; use Scenario UI LoRAs for graphical UI art.
